title: RAII and deterministic cleanup deterministic evidence bank

items.title: RAII and deterministic cleanup: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? RAII is only about memory allocated with new and does not apply to files, locks, sockets, or transactions.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. RAII applies to any resource with scoped ownership and release, including memory, files, locks, handles, and rollback guards.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. RAII applies to any resource with scoped ownership and release, including memory, files, locks, handles, and rollback guards.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: RAII applies to any resource with scoped ownership and release, including memory, files, locks, handles, and rollback guards.
RAII is only about memory allocated with new and does not apply to files, locks, sockets, or transactions.

answer.explanation: RAII applies to any resource with scoped ownership and release, including memory, files, locks, handles, and rollback guards.

items.title: RAII and deterministic cleanup: complete the language rule
items.prompt: Complete the missing CPP checkpoint in the source-aligned correction. Use the exact token or phrase required by this draft.
hints: Use the checkpoint that distinguishes the valid rule from this misconception: RAII is only about memory allocated with new and does not apply to files, locks, sockets, or transactions.

feedback.correct: Correct. The required checkpoint is 'scoped ownership'.
feedback.incorrect: Recheck the exact language rule and its boundary. RAII applies to any resource with scoped ownership and release, including memory, files, locks, handles, and rollback guards.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact CPP token or phrase that completes the reviewed rule candidate.

items.title: RAII and deterministic cleanup: executable application
items.prompt: Implement a Guard that increments a static live count in its constructor and decrements it in its destructor; print count and value inside a nested scope, then count after scope exit.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: scope-bound acquisition and deterministic destruction

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises scope-bound acquisition and deterministic destruction under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
